message.content: acquaint

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

claude --resume f6aea525-6526-42b2-99d6-caea9191ab1b
---
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

A bulleted list of the in-progress task's development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

- 0.43.0-0 plan + chores-09 plan section + todo.md ladder (done) [84]
- 0.43.0-1 src/init.rs → src/init/{mod.rs, tests.rs} (done) [84]
- 0.43.0-2 src/push.rs → src/push/{mod.rs, tests.rs, integration_tests.rs} [84]
- 0.43.0-3 src/sync.rs → src/sync/{mod.rs, tests.rs, integration_tests.rs} [84]
- 0.43.0-4 src/common.rs → src/common/{mod.rs, tests.rs} (borderline) [84]
- 0.43.0 close-out [84]

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. **Test-module extraction** across oversized files. Convert
   `src/init.rs` → `src/init/mod.rs` + `src/init/tests.rs`
   (sibling-submodule pattern; tests still reach private
   items via `use super::*;`). Same shape for `push.rs`
   (785 test lines), `sync.rs` (673), `common.rs` (361,
   borderline). Pure mechanical reshape, no behavior
   change. One sub-step per file. Active in the 0.43.0
   cycle. Splitting tests first makes a follow-on DRY walk
   across init/push/sync easier to read. [84]
1. **Ops layer / CLI decoupling.** Introduce `ops::Workspace`
   handle + per-subcommand `*Options` plain structs (flat
   fields, no clap), with `From<&XArgs> for XOptions` at
   the binary edge. Subcommand bodies become
   `fn op(ws: &Workspace, opts: &XOptions) -> Result<XOutcome, XError>`.
   Preserves leaves in `src/options_flags/` for `-h` +
   completion; eliminates `args.xxx.xxx` from op bodies.
   Multi-cycle effort; convert one subcommand end-to-end
   as worked example before sweeping. [80]
1. **forks-multi-user + bot-data-formats follow-through.**
   Design captured across two notes; concrete work to
   land when a cycle picks it up. Major pieces:
   multi-line `ochid:` parser/emitter; bot-side
   immutability enforcement; URL-shaped ochid (per-user
   / cross-repo); vendor-subdir layout
   (`.bot/<vendor>/<version>/<id>.<ext>`) +
   flat-to-vendor migration; `.claude/` → `.bot/` rename
   (gated behind symmetric `.vc-config.toml` schema).
   Each piece is its own future TODO when the design
   hardens. [82],[83]
1. **Rebase note — CLAUDE.md `### Per-file review checkpoints`.**

message.content: I had a discusion with web-claude, https://claude.ai/share/6243d643-29ea-4267-bc4a-fe112ba2d5aa, and using init/mod.rs is passe and from a practical matter with multiple subcommands they'll be module mod.rs file making fuzzy searches impractical. Intead it suggests we keep src/<mod-name>.rs but add src/<mod-name>/tests.rs. Of course now there are multiple tests.rs maybe we can have "#[cfg(<mod-name>)] mod <mod-name>_tests;" thoughts?

aiTitle: Organize Rust modules and tests structure

message.content:
I had a disscusion with web-claude, https://claude.ai/share/6243d643-29ea-4267-bc4a-fe112ba2d5aa,
and using init/mod.rs is passe and from a practical matter with multiple subcommands they'll be
module mod.rs file making fuzzy searches impractical. Instead it suggests we keep src/<mod-name>.rs
but add src/<mod-name>/tests.rs. Of course now there are multiple tests.rs maybe we can have
"#[cfg(<mod-name>)] mod <mod-name>_tests;" thoughts?

content.text:
Agree on the layout, disagree on the rename.

**Non-mod.rs is the right call.** Modern Rust (2018+) prefers `src/init.rs` + `src/init/tests.rs` over `src/init/mod.rs` — exactly because every `mod.rs` looks identical in fuzzy finders. `cargo new` even generates this style. So `src/init.rs` keeps the module's code, and `#[cfg(test)] mod tests;` resolves to `src/init/tests.rs`. No conflict — `init::tests` and `push::tests` are different namespaces.

**Don't rename to `<mod-name>_tests.rs`.** Multiple `tests.rs` files isn't the same problem as multiple `mod.rs`. `mod.rs` is an *entry point* — you open it expecting the module's main code. `tests.rs` is leaf content; fuzzy finders show the path (`init/tests.rs`, `push/tests.rs`, `sync/tests.rs`) and the directory disambiguates naturally. Renaming breaks the directory association without buying anything, and it's non-idiomatic.

**Implication for 0.43.0:** 0.43.0-1 already landed `src/init/mod.rs`. If we want consistency, that needs to be reshaped to `src/init.rs` + `src/init/tests.rs` — either as a fixup squashed into -1, or as -2 before doing push.rs. Push/sync/common would then follow the non-mod.rs pattern from the start.

Want me to lay out the fixup options for 0.43.0-1?

content.content:
200	### Goals
201	
202	- Shrink production files so the actual code is easier
203	  to navigate (`init.rs` is 2576 lines today, ~1093 of
204	  which are tests).
205	- Establish a layout shape that future cycles can keep
206	  using (further splitting into `cli.rs` / `ops.rs` per
207	  the ops-layer architecture, etc.).
208	- Done as one cycle so the four files end up
209	  consistent rather than half-converted across multiple
210	  cycles.
211	
212	### Per-file shape
213	
214	```
215	src/X.rs
216	  ↓
217	src/X/
218	  mod.rs               ← production code from src/X.rs
219	  tests.rs             ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod tests
220	  integration_tests.rs ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod
221	                         integration_tests (push, sync)
222	```
223	
224	In `src/X/mod.rs` after extraction:
225	
226	```rust
227	// ...production code...
228	
229	#[cfg(test)]
230	mod tests;
231	
232	#[cfg(test)]
233	mod integration_tests;  // only for push, sync
234	```
235	
236	In `src/X/tests.rs`:
237	
238	```rust
239	//! Unit tests for the X module.
240	
241	use super::*;
242	// ...test fns...
243	```
244	
245	### Sub-step ladder
246	
247	Size-descending order (init has the most test bulk;
248	common is borderline):
249	
250	- 0.43.0-0 plan + version bump + this section +
251	  todo.md ladder
252	- 0.43.0-1 src/init.rs → src/init/{mod.rs, tests.rs}
253	  (~1093 test lines)
254	- 0.43.0-2 src/push.rs → src/push/{mod.rs, tests.rs,
255	  integration_tests.rs} (~784 test lines + integration
256	  block)
257	- 0.43.0-3 src/sync.rs → src/sync/{mod.rs, tests.rs,
258	  integration_tests.rs} (~600 test lines, multiple
259	  `#[cfg(test)]` blocks)
260	- 0.43.0-4 src/common.rs → src/common/{mod.rs, tests.rs}
261	  (~384 test lines, borderline — call after seeing
262	  the first three land)
263	- 0.43.0 close-out
264	
265	### Order rationale
266	
267	`init` is the worked example: largest test bulk and the
268	file most-discussed in CLAUDE.md. Getting the pattern
269	right there sets the template for the others. The bot
270	thinks `common` is worth keeping for consistency but
271	reasonable to drop after seeing the first three land —
272	its 384 lines are below the threshold where extraction
273	visibly helps.
274	
275	### Per-substep contract
276	
277	Per `notes/substep-protocol.md`:
278	
279	- Run `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D
280	  warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo install --path .
281	  --locked`, retest before each sub-step commit.
282	- Bump `Cargo.toml` version at the start of each
283	  sub-step.
284	- Mark prior sub-step `(done)` and current `(current)`
285	  in todo.md ladder.
286	- Pair commits across both repos with ochid trailers.
287

file.content:
### Goals

- Shrink production files so the actual code is easier
  to navigate (`init.rs` is 2576 lines today, ~1093 of
  which are tests).
- Establish a layout shape that future cycles can keep
  using (further splitting into `cli.rs` / `ops.rs` per
  the ops-layer architecture, etc.).
- Done as one cycle so the four files end up
  consistent rather than half-converted across multiple
  cycles.

### Per-file shape

```
src/X.rs
  ↓
src/X/
  mod.rs               ← production code from src/X.rs
  tests.rs             ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod tests
  integration_tests.rs ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod
                         integration_tests (push, sync)
```

In `src/X/mod.rs` after extraction:

```rust
// ...production code...

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests;

#[cfg(test)]
mod integration_tests;  // only for push, sync
```

In `src/X/tests.rs`:

```rust
//! Unit tests for the X module.

use super::*;
// ...test fns...
```

### Sub-step ladder

Size-descending order (init has the most test bulk;
common is borderline):

- 0.43.0-0 plan + version bump + this section +
  todo.md ladder
- 0.43.0-1 src/init.rs → src/init/{mod.rs, tests.rs}
  (~1093 test lines)
- 0.43.0-2 src/push.rs → src/push/{mod.rs, tests.rs,
  integration_tests.rs} (~784 test lines + integration
  block)
- 0.43.0-3 src/sync.rs → src/sync/{mod.rs, tests.rs,
  integration_tests.rs} (~600 test lines, multiple
  `#[cfg(test)]` blocks)
- 0.43.0-4 src/common.rs → src/common/{mod.rs, tests.rs}
  (~384 test lines, borderline — call after seeing
  the first three land)
- 0.43.0 close-out

### Order rationale

`init` is the worked example: largest test bulk and the
file most-discussed in CLAUDE.md. Getting the pattern
right there sets the template for the others. The bot
thinks `common` is worth keeping for consistency but
reasonable to drop after seeing the first three land —
its 384 lines are below the threshold where extraction
visibly helps.

### Per-substep contract

Per `notes/substep-protocol.md`:

- Run `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D
  warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo install --path .
  --locked`, retest before each sub-step commit.
- Bump `Cargo.toml` version at the start of each
  sub-step.
- Mark prior sub-step `(done)` and current `(current)`
  in todo.md ladder.
- Pair commits across both repos with ochid trailers.

### Cycle exemption from per-file review

Test-module extraction is exactly the "code moving
across files" refactor case CLAUDE.md
(`### Per-file review checkpoints > Exceptions`)
exempts. Each sub-step commits as one unit; no
checkpoint between original-file edit and new-file
creation.

### Edits (this sub-step, 0.43.0-0)

- `Cargo.toml`: bump 0.42.1 → 0.43.0-0.
- `notes/chores-09.md`: this section (new).
- `notes/todo.md`: open `## In Progress` ladder for the
  cycle; mark -0 `(current)`; add `[84]` ref pointing
  here; add `[84]` to the existing Test-module
  extraction TODO entry.

## init test extraction (0.43.0-1)

Extracted `mod tests` from `src/init.rs` into sibling
`src/init/tests.rs`. Tests reach private items via
`use super::*;`; no visibility changes needed.

Production code: 1481 lines (`mod.rs`). Tests: 1091 lines
(`tests.rs`, de-indented from the 4-space wrapper indent
of the original `mod tests { ... }` block). Total +1 line
vs original (`//! Unit tests for the init module.`
header + 1 blank line in tests.rs; `mod tests;` forward
declaration + 1 blank line in mod.rs; replacing the
4-line `mod tests { ... }` wrapper).

`cargo test` shows 358 unit + 14 integration tests
passing, identical to the pre-extraction baseline.

### Edits

- `src/init.rs` → `src/init/mod.rs` (production code) +
  `src/init/tests.rs` (de-indented test body with new
  `//!` header).
- `Cargo.toml`: bump 0.43.0-0 → 0.43.0-1.
- `notes/chores-09.md`: this section (new).
- `notes/todo.md`: ladder marker flips (-0 (current) →
  (done); -1 → (done)).

## Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)

Design target for subsequent cycles: separate clap-aware
parsing from subcommand operation logic so future front-ends
(e.g. a TUI for bot-conversation exploration, or library

`args.account.account` ergonomics — renaming / accessor
shortcuts were rejected as hiding the architecture mismatch
rather than fixing it.

### Goals

- Subcommand bodies access flat fields (`opts.account`), not
  nested clap shapes (`args.account.account`).
- `src/options_flags/` leaves remain the single source of

- Per-subcommand `-h` stays bundle-specific.
- Tab completion (`vc-x1 init <TAB>`, `--account=<TAB>`)

  tests.rs             ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod tests
  integration_tests.rs ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod

// ...production code...

input.new_string:
### Per-file shape

Non-mod.rs layout (Rust 2018+ idiom): the production
file keeps its top-level path; a sibling directory holds
children. `src/X.rs` and `src/X/` coexist — Rust resolves
`mod foo;` declared in `src/X.rs` to `src/X/foo.rs`.

```
src/X.rs               ← production code (unchanged path)
src/X/
  tests.rs             ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod tests
  integration_tests.rs ← moved from #[cfg(test)] mod
                         integration_tests (push, sync)
```

In `src/X.rs` after extraction:

```rust
// ...production code...

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests;

#[cfg(test)]
mod integration_tests;  // only for push, sync
```

In `src/X/tests.rs`:

```rust
//! Unit tests for the X module.

use super::*;
// ...test fns...
```

**Why non-mod.rs.** The `mod.rs` style names every
module's entry point identically — fuzzy finders show a
column of `mod.rs` rows that the reader must
disambiguate by directory. Multiple `tests.rs` files
don't have the same pain (the file is leaf content; the
directory tells the reader which module's tests they
are). Modern Rust (`cargo new`, RFC 2126) defaults to
non-mod.rs for exactly this reason.

1	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
2	use std::process::Command;
3	
4	use clap::Args;
5	use log::{LevelFilter, debug, info, warn};
6	
7	use crate::common::{default_scope, find_workspace_root, run, scope_to_repos};
8	#[cfg(test)]
9	use crate::scope::Side;
10	use crate::scope::{Scope, parse_scope};
11	
12	/// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.
13	///
14	/// Default is `--check`: fetch + classify + report, **error** if any
15	/// repo is `behind` or `diverged`. Pass `--no-check` to actually
16	/// rebase / fast-forward. On any failure the starting state of every
17	/// repo is restored via `jj op restore`.
18	///
19	/// Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
20	/// explicitly rather than relying on the default — defaults can shift,
21	/// explicit flags lock in the contract.
22	///
23	/// Repo set is resolved from `--scope`:
24	///
25	/// - Explicit `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` — keyword forms
26	///   resolve via the project root's `.vc-config.toml`; the path
27	///   form syncs that one repo (single-repo mode).
28	/// - No `--scope` — workspace-default scope:
29	///   - dual workspace (`.vc-config.toml` with `other-repo`) → `code,bot`
30	///   - single-repo workspace (`.vc-config.toml`, no `other-repo`) → `code`

input.command: awk 'NR==604||NR==605||NR==606{print NR":"$0} NR>=748 && NR<=755{print NR":"$0} NR==1205||NR==1206{print NR":"$0}' src/sync.rs

content.content:
752	mod integration_tests {
753	    //! End-to-end tests for `sync_repos` against real dual-repo jj
754	    //! fixtures. Each test builds an isolated fixture (bare-git
755	    //! remotes + colocated jj repos) under a unique tempdir via
756	    //! `Fixture::new`, then drives the scenario with plain `jj`
757	    //! subprocess calls. Requires `jj` in `PATH`.
758	    //!
759	    //! Fixtures clean themselves up via `Fixture`'s `Drop` impl so a
760	    //! panicking test still removes its tempdir.
761	
762	    use super::*;
763	    use crate::test_helpers::Fixture;
764	    use std::fs;
765	    use std::process::Command;
766	
767	    use crate::common::{default_scope, find_workspace_root_from, scope_to_repos};
768	
769	    /// Resolver helpers must consume what `init --repo-local` produces.
770	    ///
771	    /// Builds a real dual-repo fixture (bare git remotes + colocated jj
772	    /// repos + the canonical `.vc-config.toml` pair init writes), then
773	    /// drives the resolver chain `sync()` uses when `-R` is empty:
774	    ///
775	    /// - `find_workspace_root_from(&fx.claude)` walks up to `fx.work`
776	    ///   (proves cwd-portability against init's `path = "/.claude"` /
777	    ///   `path = "/"` config split).
778	    /// - `default_scope(Some(&fx.work))` reads the workspace config
779	    ///   and resolves to the dual-repo default.
780	    /// - `scope_to_repos` maps each `Scope` shape to the right
781	    ///   absolute path(s) under the fixture.

message.content: why is line 8 of sync.rs "#[cfg(test)]"?

content.text:
It's leftover scaffolding from when `mod tests` was inline.

Originally `Side` was only referenced from inside the inline `mod tests { ... }` block, so the author gated the import to `#[cfg(test)]` to avoid an "unused import" warning in production builds. With `use super::*;` inside `mod tests`, `Side` reached the test code via the parent module's namespace.

After my extraction it still works the same way — `use super::*;` in `src/sync/tests.rs` and `src/sync/integration_tests.rs` glob-imports from `sync`, which conditionally has `Side` during test compilation. That's why nothing broke. But it's redundant: the import now belongs at the top of each test file (which are themselves `#[cfg(test)]`-gated submodules), not on the production file. I kept it as-is in -3 to stay mechanical, and noted that in the chores entry, but the cleaner state is to move it.

Want me to clean it up? Quick squash into the -3 commit:

- `src/sync.rs`: drop the `#[cfg(test)] use crate::scope::Side;` lines.
- `src/sync/tests.rs` and `src/sync/integration_tests.rs`: add `use crate::scope::Side;` directly under their existing `use super::*;`.

If yes I'll do it as a fixup squash; if you'd rather leave it, I'll just remove the chores-09 note that explains why it stayed.

content.text:
Working copy is clean — your revert restored the original state. `#[cfg(test)] use crate::scope::Side;` is back at lines 8-9 of `src/sync.rs`, matching the committed -3 revision exactly.

Your experiment confirms why I kept it: dropping the attribute alone triggers an unused-import warning (production code doesn't reference `Side`), and dropping the whole `use` line breaks the test files (their `use super::*;` was relying on `Side` being in `sync`'s namespace via the conditional import).

If you want the cleaner end-state — production file with no test-only scaffolding — the full move requires both halves:

1. Drop lines 8-9 from `src/sync.rs`.
2. Add `use crate::scope::Side;` to `src/sync/tests.rs` and `src/sync/integration_tests.rs` (right under `use super::*;`).

Want me to do that as a fixup squash into -3, or leave it and move on to -4?

message.content: Did you run the preflight pass, when changing sources we should do that, correct?

content.text:
Honest answer: I ran `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo install --path . --locked`, and `vc-x1 --version` after install — that matches the per-sub-step contract in `notes/substep-protocol.md`, but it's the **cargo cycle**, not a full preflight. Specifically, I did not run `vc-x1 sync --check` (the remote-divergence check that push's preflight runs as its first step) and did not invoke the formal preflight via `vc-x1 push --dry-run`.

Running it now to confirm state.

content.text:
Clean — `sync: 2 repos, all bookmarks up-to-date`. So no remote divergence to worry about; the squash didn't drift anything.

**Process gap to address:** the per-sub-step contract in `notes/substep-protocol.md` currently lists only the cargo cycle (fmt/clippy/test/install/retest). It doesn't include `vc-x1 sync --check`. If you want sync-check as standard before every sub-step commit (and squash), that should be codified in:

- `CLAUDE.md` Pre-commit checklist (currently items 1-9, no sync step).
- `notes/substep-protocol.md` per-sub-step "Pre-commit cargo cycle" section.

Two reasonable framings:

1. **Add `vc-x1 sync --check` as step 0 of the per-sub-step contract** (before fmt). Catches remote drift early.
2. **Use `vc-x1 push --dry-run` instead of running the cycle by hand** — it runs the formal preflight (sync + fmt + clippy + test) end-to-end, mirrors what real push will do, single command. The downside: dry-run doesn't include `cargo install --locked` retest, so we'd still want that separately.

Want me to update CLAUDE.md + substep-protocol.md with one of those (and squash into -3 alongside the Side cleanup)? Or leave the protocol alone and treat this as a one-off reminder?

## 0.43.0 close-out

Cycle landed as five separate commits on `main`
(0.43.0-0 plan + four sub-step extractions). Chose
"keep separate" over the default squash because the
per-file decomposition is informative: each sub-step
shows one production file's reshape in isolation, and
the ladder is a clean before/after for anyone reading
git log later.

### Cycle outcome

- Four production files reshaped to non-mod.rs layout
  (`src/X.rs` retained, sibling `src/X/tests.rs`
  [+ `integration_tests.rs` for push/sync]).
- Production-side line counts:
  init 1094 (+`src/init/tests.rs` 1092);
  push 1487 (+`src/push/{tests.rs 423, integration_tests.rs 359}`);
  sync 608 (+`src/sync/{tests.rs 145, integration_tests.rs 455}`);
  common 715 (+`src/common/tests.rs 385`).
- 358 unit + 14 integration tests pass identically to
  the pre-cycle baseline at every sub-step.
- `Side` import scaffolding in `sync.rs` (artifact of
  the inline `mod tests`) moved into the test files
  during the -3 fixup squash; production file no
  longer carries test-only `use` lines.

### Layout pivot mid-cycle

The original plan called for `src/X/mod.rs` per file.
After -1 landed, web-claude flagged the mod.rs idiom
as obsolete (Rust 2018+ defaults to `src/X.rs` +
`src/X/<children>.rs`), and the pivot was made before
-2. -1 was squashed to match (so all four sub-steps
share the same shape on `main`).

### Edits (this commit)

- `Cargo.toml`: bump 0.43.0-4 → 0.43.0.
- `notes/chores-09.md`: this section (new).
- `notes/todo.md`: drop the cycle's `## In Progress`
  ladder; add a `## Done` entry; remove the
  Test-module extraction `## Todo` item.

## Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)

## 0.43.0 close-out

Cycle landed as five separate commits on `main`
(0.43.0-0 plan + four sub-step extractions). Chose
"keep separate" over the default squash because the
per-file decomposition is informative: each sub-step
shows one production file's reshape in isolation, and
the ladder is a clean before/after for anyone reading
git log later.

### Cycle outcome

  (`src/X.rs` retained, sibling `src/X/tests.rs`

  push 1487 (+`src/push/{tests.rs 423, integration_tests.rs 359}`);
  sync 608 (+`src/sync/{tests.rs 145, integration_tests.rs 455}`);
  common 715 (+`src/common/tests.rs 385`).

  the pre-cycle baseline at every sub-step.

  the inline `mod tests`) moved into the test files
  during the -3 fixup squash; production file no
  longer carries test-only `use` lines.

The original plan called for `src/X/mod.rs` per file.
After -1 landed, web-claude flagged the mod.rs idiom
as obsolete (Rust 2018+ defaults to `src/X.rs` +
`src/X/<children>.rs`), and the pivot was made before
-2. -1 was squashed to match (so all four sub-steps
share the same shape on `main`).

### Edits (this commit)

- `Cargo.toml`: bump 0.43.0-4 → 0.43.0.
- `notes/chores-09.md`: this section (new).
- `notes/todo.md`: drop the cycle's `## In Progress`
  ladder; add a `## Done` entry; remove the
  Test-module extraction `## Todo` item.

## Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)

Design target for subsequent cycles: separate clap-aware
parsing from subcommand operation logic so future front-ends
(e.g. a TUI for bot-conversation exploration, or library
embedding) call the same core without retrofitting. Captures
the conclusion of the 0.42.0-4.7 side discussion about
`args.account.account` ergonomics — renaming / accessor
shortcuts were rejected as hiding the architecture mismatch
rather than fixing it.

### Goals

- Subcommand bodies access flat fields (`opts.account`), not
  nested clap shapes (`args.account.account`).
- `src/options_flags/` leaves remain the single source of
  truth for flag definition (parser, help, completer).
- Per-subcommand `-h` stays bundle-specific.
- Tab completion (`vc-x1 init <TAB>`, `--account=<TAB>`)

content.content:
525	  keeps working via clap_complete + dynamic completers.
526	- The ops layer is callable without clap as a dependency
527	  (today's `plan_init(args: &InitArgs, ...)` is not).
528	
529	### Two layers, three structures per subcommand
530	
531	- **CLI layer (clap-aware):** `InitArgs` / `CloneArgs` / etc.
532	  `#[derive(Args)]` types that flatten leaves from
533	  `src/options_flags/`. Own clap metadata for `-h` and
534	  completion. Live at the binary edge.
535	- **Ops layer (clap-free):** `InitOptions` / `CloneOptions`
536	  — plain structs, flat fields, `Default`. Plus `Workspace`,
537	  the shared platform handle (workspace root, loaded
538	  `UserConfig`, optional progress sink). Entrypoints are
539	  `ops::init(ws: &Workspace, opts: &InitOptions) -> Result<InitOutcome, InitError>`.
540	- **Boundary conversion:** `From<&InitArgs> for InitOptions`
541	  — one contained site per subcommand where leaf nesting is
542	  unpacked. Op bodies never see `args.xxx.xxx`.
543	
544	### `Workspace` ("context") vs per-op options
545	
546	- **`Workspace`:** shared platform every op runs against —
547	  workspace root, loaded user config, optional progress
548	  sink. Same shape across subcommands.
549	- **`InitOptions`** etc.: per-op input; shape differs by
550	  subcommand.
551	
552	Two parameters, not a single merged "god context." The
553	signature `fn init(ws: &Workspace, opts: &InitOptions)`
554	documents what the op depends on; a merged `Context`
555	containing every possible field would let any op silently
556	read any field with no signature-level visibility.
557	
558	If `Workspace` itself grows, escape valves (defer until
559	needed):
560	
561	- Split into `Workspace` (paths + config, read-only, cheap)
562	  vs `Session` (progress / cancellation, mutable). Most ops
563	  want `&Workspace`; long-running ops also accept
564	  `&Session`.
565	- Trait-based DI (`fn init<W: HasConfig + HasFs>(...)`) for
566	  multiple front-ends with genuinely different platform
567	  surfaces. Heavy in Rust; not the default.
568	
569	### Five rules for the ops layer
570	
571	1. **Plain options, flat fields, `Default`.** No clap types
572	   in `InitOptions`. Domain values like `RepoSelector` are
573	   fine (they're domain types); leaf wrappers like
574	   `RepoOption` are not.
575	2. **Typed errors.** `enum InitError` (likely via
576	   `thiserror`), not `Box<dyn Error>`. A TUI matches
577	   variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
578	   `Box<dyn Error>` discards exactly the information a GUI
579	   most needs.
580	3. **Returned outcomes, not `println!`.** Each op returns a
581	   structured result; CLI formats it for stdout, TUI
582	   populates panels. Library writes nothing to stdout
583	   itself.
584	4. **Progress via a sink.** Long-running ops accept an
585	   optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` (or
586	   `mpsc::Sender<Event>`). CLI installs a stderr sink; TUI
587	   a status-bar sink; tests a recording sink. `log` /
588	   `tracing` covers diagnostics but is lossy for structured
589	   progress.
590	5. **No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in ops.**
591	   Everything an op needs goes in `Workspace` or
592	   `*Options`. CLI resolves cwd/env once at startup and
593	   builds the handle. This is what lets a multi-window TUI
594	   drive multiple workspaces without cross-contamination.
595	
596	### Completion stays at the clap layer
597	
598	- Static structure (`-h`, subcommand layout, flag presence):
599	  clap derive on `InitArgs` + leaves. Unchanged from today.
600	- Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>` against
601	  accounts in user config): `ArgValueCompleter` attached to
602	  the leaf in `src/options_flags/`. Already enabled via
603	  `clap_complete`'s `unstable-dynamic` feature. The leaf is
604	  the right home — completion is a clap-aware concern with
605	  no place in `InitOptions`.
606	
607	Ops layer stays clap-free; leaf layer stays clap-aware.
608	Completion drives nothing in the ops layer.
609	
610	### The contained wart
611	
612	`From<&InitArgs> for InitOptions` walks leaf nesting once
613	per subcommand:
614	
615	```rust
616	impl From<&InitArgs> for InitOptions {
617	    fn from(a: &InitArgs) -> Self {
618	        Self {
619	            target: a.target.clone(),
620	            account: a.account.account.clone(),
621	            scope: a.scope.scope,
622	            private: a.provision.private.private,
623	            // ...
624	        }

   variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
   `Box<dyn Error>` discards exactly the information a GUI
   most needs.
3. **Returned outcomes, not `println!`.** Each op returns a
   structured result; CLI formats it for stdout, TUI
   populates panels. Library writes nothing to stdout
   itself.
4. **Progress via a sink.** Long-running ops accept an
   optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` (or
   `mpsc::Sender<Event>`). CLI installs a stderr sink; TUI
   a status-bar sink; tests a recording sink. `log` /
   `tracing` covers diagnostics but is lossy for structured
   progress.
5. **No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in ops.**
   Everything an op needs goes in `Workspace` or
   `*Options`. CLI resolves cwd/env once at startup and
   builds the handle. This is what lets a multi-window TUI
   drive multiple workspaces without cross-contamination.

### Completion stays at the clap layer

- Static structure (`-h`, subcommand layout, flag presence):
  clap derive on `InitArgs` + leaves. Unchanged from today.
- Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>` against
  accounts in user config): `ArgValueCompleter` attached to
  the leaf in `src/options_flags/`. Already enabled via
  `clap_complete`'s `unstable-dynamic` feature. The leaf is
  the right home — completion is a clap-aware concern with
  no place in `InitOptions`.

Ops layer stays clap-free; leaf layer stays clap-aware.
Completion drives nothing in the ops layer.

### The contained wart

`From<&InitArgs> for InitOptions` walks leaf nesting once
per subcommand:

```rust

    fn from(a: &InitArgs) -> Self {
        Self {
            target: a.target.clone(),
            account: a.account.account.clone(),
            scope: a.scope.scope,
            private: a.provision.private.private,
            // ...
        }

Ok, next on todo is Ops layer architecture, basically the implementation of a
subcommand is now passed XxxxArgs, Instead it should be passed a Context

content.text:
Read the section. Your instinct is close to what chores-09 lands on, but the design splits "context" into two structs rather than one:

- **`Workspace`** — paths + loaded `UserConfig`, built once at CLI startup, **same shape across all subcommands** (this is your "Context").
- **`XOptions`** — per-op input, plain struct with flat fields, built `From<&XArgs>` (this is essentially the trait you're sketching, except its output type differs per subcommand). Body becomes `fn init(ws: &Workspace, opts: &InitOptions)`.

The two-struct argument: a single uniform Context holding everything would let any op silently read any field — `fn init(ctx: &Context)` doesn't tell a reader what `init` actually depends on, whereas `fn init(ws: &Workspace, opts: &InitOptions)` does. The price is one `From<&XArgs>` impl per subcommand (clap nesting → flat fields, e.g. `opts.account` not `args.account.account`).

Two paths from here:
1. Accept the two-struct shape and start a 0.44.0 cycle: introduce `Workspace`, port one subcommand end-to-end as the worked example (sync or chid is lighter than init), then sweep.
2. Push back on the split — argue for a unified `Context` and revise chores-09 first.

Which direction do you want to go?

message.content:
I don't like Workspace that's a term the bot seems to favor for logs of thing but in
rust a Workspace is a formal thing so a no from me. I suggest Context, but your're
welcome to suggest other names. Also, after thinking about we should try the two
parameters as they are separate concers, so I suggest Context and <mod-name>OptFlags.
Thoughts

I don't like Workspace that's a term the bot seems to favor for lots of things, but in
rust a Workspace is a formal thing so a no from me. I suggest Context, but your're
welcome to suggest other names. Also, after thinking about we should try the two

Thoughts

content.text:
Agreed on dropping Workspace — Cargo owns the term formally, and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`). Doubly overloaded.

**`Context` is fine.** Generic but idiomatic in Rust (`std::task::Context`, `tower::Context`, `anyhow::Context`). If you want something more descriptive, `Project` reads true to what it'd actually hold (dual-repo project root + `UserConfig`), but `Context` is conventional and shorter.

**Pushing back gently on `<mod-name>OptFlags`.** The codebase already has `src/options_flags/` for clap-aware leaf types (`AccountOption`, `RepoOption`, `ScopeOption`, …). Naming the clap-*free* per-op struct `InitOptFlags` collides visually with that directory and blurs the boundary the cycle is trying to establish. The chores-09 sketch's `InitOptions` (or shorter `InitOpts`) keeps the split readable: leaves stay in `options_flags/`, the flat ops-layer struct gets a different word. Also worth noting: since the ops layer is meant to be callable from non-CLI front-ends (TUI, library embedding), naming the input "OptFlags" fixes the wrong frame — it isn't always from flags.

So my pick: `Context` + `InitOpts` (or `InitOptions`). Want to go with that, or try a different combo?

## Init ops port (0.44.0)

Single-step cycle: introduce `Context` + `InitParams` and
port `init` to the new shape. Establishes the worked
example for the design captured in
[Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)](#ops-layer-architecture-forward-looking)
above; remaining subcommands defer to later cycles.

### Naming differs from the design section above

Written before this cycle, the design section uses
`Workspace` and `XOptions`. This cycle adopts:

- `Workspace` → `Context`. Cargo owns "workspace"
  formally, and this codebase already uses "workspace"
  for the dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
  Doubly overloaded.
- `XOptions` → `XParams`. Avoids collision with Rust's
  `Option<T>` (`InitParams` fields are a mix of required
  and `Option<T>`-typed, which `Options` muddles).
  Doesn't collide with `src/options_flags/` either.

The forward-looking design section is left as-is for
this cycle; once 0.44.0 lands and the worked example
is in tree, that section can be retitled / updated
during a future close-out so the codebase has one
naming.

### Why init

`args.account.account` ergonomics — the trigger for the
whole design — lives here; init also has the deepest
leaf nesting (account / repo / scope / private /
template / push retry). If the pattern survives init,
sync / chid / push / clone port trivially.

### In scope

- `src/context.rs` (new): `Context` struct holding the
  workspace root path + loaded `UserConfig`. Built once
  at startup.
- `src/init/params.rs` (new): `InitParams` flat plain
  struct (owns its values) + `impl From<&InitArgs> for
  InitParams`.
- `src/init.rs`: port `plan_init` and any other
  init-side `pub fn` entrypoints to
  `fn (&Context, &InitParams)`. Body reads
  `params.account` instead of `args.account.account`.
- `src/main.rs`: build `Context` once at startup; the
  init dispatch arm passes `(&ctx, &(&args).into())`.
- `src/init/tests.rs`: pure-unit tests construct
  `Context` + `InitParams` directly (no clap
  `try_parse_from` plumbing); integration paths still
  exercise the clap edge.

### Out of scope (deferred)

- Sweep across sync / chid / push / clone / etc.
  Each is its own later cycle.
- Per chores-09 "five rules": typed errors, returned
  outcomes (vs `println!`), `ProgressSink`. Keep this
  cycle to the structural split only.
- `Context` fields beyond workspace root + user
  config. Defer until a real consumer surfaces.

### Pre-commit checklist

Standard: `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --
-D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo install --path .
--locked`, retest.

### Edits (filled in at commit time)

- `Cargo.toml`: bump 0.43.0 → 0.44.0.
- `src/context.rs` (new).
- `src/init/params.rs` (new); declare `mod params;` in
  `src/init.rs`.
- `src/init.rs`: ported function signatures + bodies.
- `src/main.rs`: `Context` build site + init call.
- `src/init/tests.rs`: tests rewritten against the
  new signatures.
- `notes/chores-09.md`: this section.
- `notes/todo.md`: update the Ops-layer entry — init
  ported, sweep remaining.

681	## InitParams implementation (0.44.0)
682	
683	Single-step cycle: introduce `Context` + `InitParams` and
684	port `init` to the new shape. Establishes the worked
685	example for the design captured in
686	[Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)](#ops-layer-architecture-forward-looking)
687	above; remaining subcommands defer to later cycles.
688	
689	### Naming differs from "Ops layer architecture" section above
690	
691	Written before this cycle, the design section uses
692	`Workspace` and `XOptions`. This cycle adopts:
693	
694	- `Workspace` → `Context`. Cargo owns "workspace"
695	  formally, and this codebase already uses "workspace"
696	  for the dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
697	  Doubly overloaded.
698	- `XOptions` → `XParams`. Avoids collision with Rust's
699	  `Option<T>` (`InitParams` fields are a mix of required
700	  and `Option<T>`-typed, which `Options` muddles).
701	  Doesn't collide with `src/options_flags/` either.
702	
703	The forward-looking design section is left as-is for
704	this cycle; once 0.44.0 lands and the worked example
705	is in tree, that section can be retitled / updated
706	during a future close-out so the codebase has one
707	naming.

file.content:
## InitParams implementation (0.44.0)

Single-step cycle: introduce `Context` + `InitParams` and
port `init` to the new shape. Establishes the worked
example for the design captured in
[Ops layer architecture (forward-looking)](#ops-layer-architecture-forward-looking)
above; remaining subcommands defer to later cycles.

### Naming differs from "Ops layer architecture" section above

Written before this cycle, the design section uses
`Workspace` and `XOptions`. This cycle adopts:

- `Workspace` → `Context`. Cargo owns "workspace"
  formally, and this codebase already uses "workspace"
  for the dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
  Doubly overloaded.
- `XOptions` → `XParams`. Avoids collision with Rust's
  `Option<T>` (`InitParams` fields are a mix of required
  and `Option<T>`-typed, which `Options` muddles).
  Doesn't collide with `src/options_flags/` either.

The forward-looking design section is left as-is for
this cycle; once 0.44.0 lands and the worked example
is in tree, that section can be retitled / updated
during a future close-out so the codebase has one
naming.

### Why init

content.text:
Title change reads better — "InitParams implementation" leads with what's being built; subsection rename is more navigable. Both improvements. Going.

First two edits per the new process: Cargo.toml bump + todo.md In Progress marker. Then read the init surface to plan the actual port.

content.content:
21	pub struct InitArgs {
22	    /// Target — URL, owner/name shorthand, path, or bare NAME.
23	    ///
24	    /// - URL: `git@host:owner/name(.git)?`, `https://...(.git)?`
25	    ///   — used as-is; config not consulted.
26	    /// - owner/name shorthand: resolves to
27	    ///   `git@github.com:owner/name.git`; config not consulted.
28	    /// - Path: `./X`, `../X`, `/X`, `~/X`, `~`, `.`, `..` — is the
29	    ///   directory path; remote resolved via `--repo` chain.
30	    /// - Bare NAME: becomes NAME.git; remote resolved via
31	    ///   `--repo` chain.
32	    #[arg(value_name = "TARGET", verbatim_doc_comment)]
33	    pub target: String,
34	
35	    /// Repo directory name override (URL / owner/name forms only).
36	    ///
37	    /// - URL / owner/name forms: repo created at `cwd/<NAME>`
38	    ///   instead of the URL-derived name.
39	    /// - Path / bare-NAME forms: error if given (TARGET already
40	    ///   names the repo).
41	    #[arg(value_name = "NAME", verbatim_doc_comment)]
42	    pub name: Option<String>,
43	
44	    /// `--account` — flatten of the shared [`AccountOption`] leaf.
45	    #[command(flatten)]
46	    pub account: AccountOption,
47	
48	    /// `--repo` — flatten of the shared [`RepoOption`] leaf.
49	    /// Init's built-in categories: `remote` (URL prefix; init
50	    /// appends `/<NAME>.git`) and `local` (parent dir for
51	    /// fixture bare repos). Meaningful only with Path or
52	    /// bare-NAME targets.
53	    #[command(flatten)]
54	    pub repo: RepoOption,
55	
56	    /// `--scope` — flatten of the shared [`ScopeOption`] leaf.
57	    #[command(flatten)]
58	    pub scope: ScopeOption,
59	
60	    /// `--dry-run` + `--private` + `--push-retries` /
61	    /// `--push-retry-delay` — flatten of the shared
62	    /// [`ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`] bundle.
63	    #[command(flatten)]
64	    pub provision: ProvisionOptionFlagBundle,
65	
66	    /// `--use-template` — flatten of the shared
67	    /// [`UseTemplateOption`] leaf.
68	    #[command(flatten)]
69	    pub use_template: UseTemplateOption,
70	
71	    /// `--config none|<path>` — flatten of the shared
72	    /// [`ConfigOption`] leaf. Only meaningful with `--scope=por`;
73	    /// rejected at preflight when paired with `--scope=code,bot`.
74	    /// `.gitignore` is always written regardless of `--config`.
75	    #[command(flatten)]
76	    pub config: ConfigOption,
77	}
78	
79	/// Run a command with retries, sleeping between attempts.
80	fn run_retry(
81	    cmd: &str,
82	    args: &[&str],
83	    cwd: &Path,
84	    retry: &PushRetryOptions,
85	) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
86	    let mut last_err = String::new();
87	    for attempt in 1..=retry.push_retries {
88	        match run(cmd, args, cwd) {
89	            Ok(out) => {
90	                if attempt > 1 {
91	                    debug!("succeeded after {attempt} attempts");
92	                }
93	                return Ok(out);
94	            }
95	            Err(e) => {
96	                last_err = e.to_string();
97	                if attempt < retry.push_retries {
98	                    debug!(
99	                        "attempt {attempt}/{} failed: {last_err}",
100	                        retry.push_retries
101	                    );
102	                    debug!("retrying in {}s...", retry.push_retry_delay);
103	                    std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_secs(retry.push_retry_delay));
104	                }
105	            }

content.content:
567	/// - `Path(p)` → `p` is the destination; basename names the repo;
568	///   remote URL resolved from config via the `--repo` chain.
569	/// - `BareName(n)` → destination at `cwd/<n>`; remote resolved
570	///   from config.
571	///
572	/// The `cfg` parameter is the loaded user config; `init` loads it
573	/// once at entry and passes it through so tests can supply a
574	/// synthetic config without touching disk.
575	pub(crate) fn plan_init(
576	    args: &InitArgs,
577	    cfg: &UserConfig,
578	) -> Result<InitPlan, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
579	    debug!(
580	        "init args: target={:?}, name={:?}, account={:?}, repo={:?}, scope={:?}, private={}",
581	        args.target,
582	        args.name,
583	        args.account.account,
584	        args.repo.repo,
585	        args.scope.scope,
586	        args.provision.private.private
587	    );
588	
589	    let scope = match args.scope.scope {
590	        ScopeKind::CodeBot => Scope::Roles(vec![Side::Code, Side::Bot]),
591	        ScopeKind::Por => Scope::Roles(vec![Side::Code]),
592	    };
593	
594	    if scope.is_code_only()
595	        && let Some(t) = &args.use_template.use_template
596	        && t.contains(',')
597	    {
598	        return Err(format!(
599	            "--scope=por takes a single template path; got '{t}' (drop the `,BOT` half)"
600	        )
601	        .into());
602	    }
603	
604	    if args.config.raw.is_some() && args.scope.scope == ScopeKind::CodeBot {
605	        return Err(
606	            "--config is only valid with --scope=por (dual-mode configs are per-side and unconditional)".into(),
607	        );
608	    }
609	    if let Some(ConfigKind::Path(p)) = args.config.resolve(ConfigKind::None)
610	        && !p.exists()
611	    {
612	        return Err(format!("--config: path does not exist: {}", p.display()).into());
613	    }
614	
615	    let parsed = parse_target(&args.target)?;
616	    debug!("parse_target: {:?} → {:?}", args.target, parsed);
617	    let plan = match parsed {
618	        Target::Url(url) => plan_from_url(args, scope, url),
619	        Target::OwnerName(o, n) => {
620	            plan_from_url(args, scope, format!("git@github.com:{o}/{n}.git"))
621	        }
622	        Target::Path(p) => plan_from_path(args, scope, p, cfg),
623	        Target::BareName(n) => plan_from_bare_name(args, scope, n, cfg),
624	    }?;
625	    debug!(
626	        "plan_init: project_dir={}, name={}, code_url={}, provisioner={:?}, gh_code_slug={:?}, code_bare_path={:?}, session_url={:?}",
627	        plan.project_dir.display(),
628	        plan.name,
629	        plan.code_url,
630	        plan.provisioner,
631	        plan.gh_code_slug,
632	        plan.code_bare_path,
633	        plan.session_url,
634	    );
635	    Ok(plan)
636	}
637	
638	/// Plan when TARGET is a URL or `owner/name` shorthand.
639	///
640	/// - URL is explicit; config not consulted.
641	/// - `--account` / `--repo` are rejected (would have no effect).

input.old_string:
### Edits (filled in at commit time)

- `Cargo.toml`: bump 0.43.0 → 0.44.0.
- `src/context.rs` (new).
- `src/init/params.rs` (new); declare `mod params;` in
  `src/init.rs`.
- `src/init.rs`: ported function signatures + bodies.
- `src/main.rs`: `Context` build site + init call.
- `src/init/tests.rs`: tests rewritten against the
  new signatures.
- `notes/chores-09.md`: this section.
- `notes/todo.md`: update the Ops-layer entry — init
  ported, sweep remaining.
input.new_string:
### Edits

- `Cargo.toml`: bump 0.43.0 → 0.44.0.
- `src/context.rs` (new): `Context` struct holding
  `user_config: UserConfig` + `Context::load()` ctor;
  declared `mod context;` in `src/main.rs`.
- `src/init/params.rs` (new): `InitParams` flat struct
  + `impl From<&InitArgs> for InitParams` (production
  default sets `create_symlink: true`); declared
  `mod params; pub use params::InitParams;` in
  `src/init.rs`.
- `src/init.rs`: `pub fn init` signature changed
  from `init(args: &InitArgs, create_symlink: bool)`
  to `init(ctx: &Context, params: &InitParams)`
  (`create_symlink` folded into `InitParams`).
  Internal helpers (`plan_init`, `plan_from_url`,
  `plan_from_path`, `plan_from_bare_name`,
  `plan_remote`, `plan_local`, `build_plan`,
  `create_por`, `create_dual`, `push_repo`,
  `run_remote_step`) all switched from
  `args: &InitArgs` to `params: &InitParams`. Body
  reads `params.account` instead of
  `args.account.account` (and similarly across all
  leaf nestings). `args.config.resolve(ConfigKind::None)`
  call sites collapse to `&params.config` since the
  resolve runs at the boundary now. `cfg` inside
  `pub fn init` switched from `config::load()?` to
  `&ctx.user_config`.
- `src/main.rs`: `mod context;` added; init dispatch
  builds `Context::load()` then converts
  `InitParams::from(&init_args)` and calls
  `init::init(&ctx, &params)`.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`: `Fixture` and `FixturePor`
  builders updated — load `Context`, build
  `InitParams::from(&args)`, override
  `params.create_symlink = false`, then call
  `init(&ctx, &params)`. Imports updated to bring
  `Context` and `InitParams` into scope.
- `src/init/tests.rs`: every `plan_init(&args, ...)`
  call site rewritten to
  `plan_init(&InitParams::from(&args), ...)`. The two
  `plan_init(&args_for(...), ...)` sites split across
  multiple lines for readability. Tests of `InitArgs`
  (parse/defaults) untouched — those exercise the
  clap edge.
- `notes/chores-09.md`: this section + the
  `## InitParams implementation (0.44.0)` plan
  section.
- `notes/todo.md`: drop the cycle's `## In Progress`
  entry; add `## Done` entry; rewrite the
  `Ops layer / CLI decoupling` `## Todo` item to
  reflect "init done, sweep remaining."

content.text:
0.44.0 InitParams cycle complete. Pre-push summary:

**New files:**
- `src/context.rs` (29 lines): `Context { user_config: UserConfig }` + `Context::load()` ctor.
- `src/init/params.rs` (70 lines): flat `InitParams` + `impl From<&InitArgs> for InitParams` (production sets `create_symlink: true`).

**Ported:**
- `src/init.rs`: `pub fn init` now `(ctx: &Context, params: &InitParams)` (`create_symlink` folded into params). All 10 internal helpers (`plan_init`, planners, creators, push helpers) switched from `&InitArgs` to `&InitParams`. ~30 `args.xxx.xxx` access sites flattened to `params.xxx`. `cfg` source switched from `config::load()?` to `&ctx.user_config`. `args.config.resolve(...)` sites collapse to `&params.config` (resolve runs at boundary).
- `src/main.rs`: `mod context;` + init dispatch builds Context, converts `InitParams::from(&init_args)`, calls `init::init(&ctx, &params)`.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`: both `Fixture` builders load Context, build `InitParams::from(&args)`, override `create_symlink = false`, call ported `init`.
- `src/init/tests.rs`: every `plan_init(&args, …)` site now `plan_init(&InitParams::from(&args), …)`. clap-edge tests untouched.

**Notes:**
- `notes/chores-09.md`: full edit list filled into `## InitParams implementation (0.44.0)`.
- `notes/todo.md`: In Progress entry dropped, Done entry added, Ops-layer `## Todo` rewritten to reflect "init done, sweep remaining."

**Verification:** fmt clean, clippy `--all-targets -- -D warnings` clean, `cargo test` 358 unit + 14 integration (baseline preserved), `cargo install --path . --locked` succeeded, `vc-x1 --version` reports 0.44.0.

Work complete. Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

lastPrompt: Looks good, now I'd like test using a "Fixture" how can I create a test Fixture?

aiTitle: Organize Rust modules and tests structure

content.text:
Fixture infrastructure is already wired up — `src/test_helpers.rs > pub struct Fixture` runs the real `init(&ctx, &params)` in a tempdir and gives you back the populated paths.

**API in two lines:**
- `Fixture::new(tag)` — dual-repo (`code,bot`).
- `FixturePor::new(tag)` — single-repo (POR / `--scope=por`).

Both auto-clean on drop unless you set `VC_X1_TEST_KEEP=1` (then the tempdir is preserved for inspection — printed to stderr).

**Returns:**
- `fx.base` — root tempdir (also holds `<base>/remote-code.git` and `<base>/remote-claude.git` bare remotes).
- `fx.work` — code repo (`<base>/work`).
- `fx.claude` — session repo (`<base>/work/.claude`).
- `fx.repos()` — `vec![work, claude]` for `sync_repos` etc.

**Canonical use, in a `#[cfg(test)]` module under whichever subcommand you're testing:**

```rust
#[test]
fn init_dual_creates_layout() {
    let fx = crate::test_helpers::Fixture::new("init-dual-layout");
    assert!(fx.work.join(".jj").exists());
    assert!(fx.claude.join(".jj").exists());
    assert!(fx.work.join(".vc-config.toml").exists());
    // … exercise the thing you're verifying
}
```

**Variants:**
- `Fixture::new_opts(tag, with_pending, use_template)` — `with_pending=true` writes a TODO.md / session-notes.md so `@` carries uncommitted changes (good for finalize / push tests). `use_template: Option<String>` is the `CODE[,BOT]` value forwarded to init.
- `FixturePor::new_with_config(tag, Some(path))` — exercises the `--config <path>` POR path.

**Where to put the test:**
- Under `src/init/tests.rs` if it's a unit-level check (calls private fns or asserts on internal state).
- Under `src/init/integration_tests.rs` (new file — init doesn't have one yet) if it's end-to-end and you want it grouped like push/sync's integration suites.
- Under `tests/<name>.rs` if it's a top-level CLI smoke test (only sees the public binary).

What are you trying to verify? I can sketch the right shape based on what you're after.

content: Goal: 0.44.0 InitParams cycle — port init to `Context` + `InitParams`. Status: code change complete, tests pass at `0.44.0-dev1`, awaiting your review before commit. Next: review the diff, then I draft the commit message.

content.text:
At-commit-time edits applied (Cargo.toml → 0.44.0; In Progress dropped; Done entry added; Ops-layer Todo rewritten). Proposed commit message:

**$TITLE**
```
refactor: introduce Context + InitParams (0.44.0)
```

**$BODY**
```
Introduce shared `Context` (loaded UserConfig) and flat
`InitParams` (clap-free) at the binary edge; port `init`
and all internal helpers to take `(&Context, &InitParams)`.
Op body reads `params.account` instead of
`args.account.account`. Worked example for the broader
ops-layer architecture; sweep across remaining
subcommands deferred to later cycles.

Validated end-to-end against a real GitHub target:
init → repo created with templates; clone of that
repo back; validate-desc on both sides clean.

- Cargo.toml: bump 0.43.0 → 0.44.0.
- src/context.rs (new): Context { user_config } +
  load() ctor.
- src/init/params.rs (new): InitParams flat struct +
  From<&InitArgs> (production sets create_symlink: true).
- src/init.rs: pub fn init signature switched to
  (&Context, &InitParams); 10 internal helpers ported
  from &InitArgs to &InitParams; ~30 args.xxx.xxx sites
  flattened to params.xxx; cfg sourced from
  &ctx.user_config.
- src/main.rs: mod context; init dispatch builds
  Context::load() and InitParams::from(&init_args).
- src/test_helpers.rs: Fixture / FixturePor builders
  load Context, build InitParams, override
  create_symlink=false, call init(&ctx, &params).
- src/init/tests.rs: every plan_init(&args, ...) call
  rewritten to plan_init(&InitParams::from(&args), ...).
- notes/chores-09.md: ## InitParams implementation
  (0.44.0) plan + edits sections.
- notes/todo.md: drop In Progress entry; add Done
  entry; rewrite Ops-layer Todo to "init done, sweep
  remaining."
```
